LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT AND NOTICE OF SUBMISSION IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 6, 2014 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 6, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT ONLY. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Alisa J. Kim, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff, American Express Bank, FSB, a Federal Savings Bank,
Order Granting Waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Fredrick E. Leidecker, aka Fred E. Leidecker, individually and DBA Meadow Park Plaza,
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
Damages: $16,005.37
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0
Attorney's Fees: $0
Costs: $515.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $16,520.37

Judgment signed in open court.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Entering Judgment And Ntc Of Submission
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB, A FEDERAL SAVINGS BANK's motion For Order Entering Judgment is off calendar. The moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers or a proof of service in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Entering Judgment And Ntc Of Submission
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Continued To Jun-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB'S Motion For Order Entering Judgment And Notice Of Submission is continued to June 6, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 6, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the proposed judgment only. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
